name: STANLEY BLACK & DECKER, INC.
business: Stanley Black & Decker is a global provider of hand tools, power tools, outdoor products and related accessories, as well as a leading provider of engineered fastening solutions, with 2025 consolidated annual revenues of $15.1 billion. The Tools & Outdoor segment sells power tools, hand tools, accessories, storage, and outdoor power equipment. The Engineered Fastening segment sells highly engineered components such as fasteners and fittings to the automotive, manufacturing, electronics, construction, and aerospace industries.

exposures.sentence: For example, in April 2025, China restricted export of certain rare earth minerals and may in the future continue to restrict, expand restrictions, or stop exporting these or other materials.

The Outdoor product line primarily sells corded and cordless electric lawn and garden products, including hedge trimmers, string trimmers, lawn mowers, pressure washers and related accessories, and gas powered lawn and garden products, including lawn tractors, zero turn ride on mowers, walk behind mowers, snow blowers, residential robotic mowers, hand-held outdoor power equipment, garden tools, and parts and accessories to professionals and consumers primarily under the DEWALT , CRAFTSMAN , CUB CADET , BLACK+DECKER , and HUSTLER brand names.

The business sells to customers in the automotive, manufacturing, electronics, construction, and aerospace industries, amongst others, and its products are distributed through a direct sales force and, to a lesser extent, third-party distributors.

The Company's future growth rate depends upon a number of factors, including its ability to (i) identify and evolve with emerging technological and broader industry trends in its target end-markets, including, but not limited to, artificial intelligence machine learning and robotics; (ii) defend its market share against an ever-expanding number of competitors, including many new and non-traditional competitors; (iii) monitor disruptive technologies and business models; and (iv) attract, develop, and retain individuals with the requisite technical expertise and understanding of customers needs to develop new technologies and introduce new products.

The Company continues to invest in its innovation model targeting key end-user segments with products designed to deliver against the attributes of productivity, quality and safety, and places an emphasis on electrification.

catalysts.event: Sale of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing (CAM) business to Howmet Aerospace
catalysts.sentence: The sale is subject to regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions and is expected to close in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Achieve annual net cost savings of $188 million from 2025 restructuring actions
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects to achieve annual net cost savings of approximately $188 million by the end of 2026 related to the restructuring costs incurred during 2025.

catalysts.event: Settlement of forward stock purchase contract
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the Company amended the settlement date to June 2028, or earlier at the Company's option.
